html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 羁棲 - Compound meaning 'to lodge temporarily' or 'to stay as a guest,' often with a sense of being in a foreign or unfamiliar place.
  • 客来闲话 - Refers to guests coming to chat casually.
  • 苦气弱不能剧谈 - Means 'struggling with weak health and unable to engage in intense conversation.'
  • 以笔代舌 - Metaphor for using writing to express oneself instead of speaking.
  • 自夏徂秋 - Temporal phrase meaning 'from summer to autumn,' indicating a period of time.
  • 积成卷帙 - Means 'accumulated into volumes,' referring to the collection of writings.
  • 熙朝掌故 - Refers to historical anecdotes or records from the prosperous era.
  • 往代轶闻 - Means 'anecdotes from past dynasties,' referring to stories from earlier times.
  • 两别风之舛 - Refers to discrepancies or errors in different versions of the same story or text.
  • 弄獐伏猎之讹 - Refers to errors or misinterpretations in historical records or stories.
  • 急我尤勤焉 - Means 'especially diligent in helping me,' indicating a high level of assistance.
  • 辄题数语 - Means 'to write a few words' or 'to jot down a few remarks.'
  • 募资刊之 - Means 'to raise funds for publication.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道光丁亥 - Refers to the year 1847 during the Daoguang era of the Qing dynasty.
  • 武林 - Refers to Hangzhou, often called 'Wulin' in classical texts.
  • 柳泉太守 - Refers to a magistrate named Liuquan, likely a historical figure or a pseudonym.
  • 郡斋 - Refers to the magistrate's office or study.
  • 子寿 - Likely refers to a scholar or friend named Zishou, who provided historical references.
  • 七十三翁 - Refers to the author, Xu Zhongyuan, who was 73 years old at the time.
  • 许仲元 - The author's name, Xu Zhongyuan, a Qing dynasty scholar.

Proper Nouns

  • 道光丁亥 - The year 1847 during the Daoguang era.
  • 武林 - Hangzhou.
  • 柳泉太守 - A magistrate named Liuquan.
  • 郡斋 - The magistrate's office or study.
  • 子寿 - A scholar or friend named Zishou.
  • 许仲元 - The author's name, Xu Zhongyuan.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三异笔谈卷一 - Title referring to three unusual events and the first volume of notes.
  • 郡人以里第分，呼曰东王西王 - People of the prefecture divide the Wang family into 'East Wang' and 'West Wang' based on their neighborhoods.
  • 前明时已簪缨相继 - During the Ming dynasty, the Wang family held official positions one after another.
  • 胜国并无科第 - In the previous dynasty, the Wang family had no members who passed the imperial examinations.
  • 科第自农山侍御始 - The Wang family's success in the imperial examinations began with 'Nongshan Shiyu'.
  • 武英总宪，司农兄弟皆侍御子也 - 'Wuying Zongxian' and 'Sinao' are the sons of 'Shiyu'.
  • 寄鞠于叔处 - 'Shiyu' was entrusted to his uncle's care.
  • 弱冠补诸生 - 'Shiyu' was selected as a student at the age of 20.
  • 寄于学宫 - 'Shiyu' was entrusted to the care of the school.
  • 村翁姚姓 - An elderly man from the village with the surname Yao.
  • 延之课子 - The village elder invited 'Shiyu' to teach his son.
  • 暑月，黎明赴馆 - 'Shiyu' went to the teaching hall at dawn during the summer months.
  • 翁方登楼观稼 - The village elder climbed a tower to observe the crops.
  • 隐约间，有双灯前导而来者 - The village elder saw two lights leading someone in the distance.
  • 讶而迹之 - The village elder was surprised and followed the person.
  • 稍近则灯隐 - The lights disappeared as the person got closer.
  • 塾师至 - The tutor arrived.
  • 晚复觇焉 - The village elder spied again in the evening.
  • 才越一阡 - The village elder just crossed one thousand steps.
  • 月影黄昏中 - The moonlight at dusk.
  • 复有双灯前引矣 - The village elder saw two lights leading the way again.
  • 意其必贵 - The village elder thought the person must be noble.
  • 家有及笄女 - The village elder's family has a daughter of marriageable age.
  • 遣人与其叔议婚 - The village elder sent someone to discuss marriage with his uncle.
  • 叔以贫辞 - The uncle refused because he was poor.
  • 强而后可 - He agreed after being forced.
  • 遂赘焉 - He then married into the family.
  • 逾数年翁殁 - After several years, the village elder died.
  • 姻娅中颇有加白眼者 - Many relatives gave him a cold look.
  • 姚夫人谓侍御曰 - Lady Yao said to the tutor.
  • 婿本赘也，翁亡何赘 - The son-in-law originally married into the family, and the elder died, so why marry into.
  • 盍归乎 - Why not return home.
  • 我乃无家 - He has no home.
  • 未婚时岂露处耶 - Before marriage, how could he live in the open.
  • 寄斋夫舍 - He was entrusted to the care of the caretaker's house.
  • 何不即与斋夫商之 - Why not immediately discuss it with the caretaker.
  • 赁一庑楼棲焉 - He rented a small building to live in.

Proper Nouns

  • 茸城王氏 - The Wang family of Rongcheng.
  • 农山侍御 - A proper name, the beginning of the Wang family's success in the imperial examinations.
  • 武英总宪 - A title or name, one of the sons of 'Shiyu'.
  • 司农 - A title or name, one of the sons of 'Shiyu'.
  • 村翁姚姓 - An elderly man from the village with the surname Yao.
  • 姚夫人 - Lady Yao.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 挈二子先往 - Literally means 'to take two sons ahead,' implying a sense of urgency or preparation.
  • 司农方在妊 - '司农' refers to the official in charge of agriculture or granaries, and '方在妊' means 'is currently pregnant.'
  • 仓惶中胎震欲娩 - Describes a situation of panic ('仓惶') where the pregnant woman feels her baby is about to be born ('胎震欲娩').
  • 急避人家竹园中 - Indicates a hurried retreat to someone's bamboo garden.
  • 朦胧见一白衣老媪 - Describes a vague or hazy vision of an elderly woman dressed in white.
  • 为之扶掖 - Means 'to support or assist her.'
  • 砺甲断脐 - Refers to the act of cutting the umbilical cord, possibly using a sharp object ('砺甲').
  • 裂裳作褓 - Means 'to tear clothing to make a swaddling cloth.'
  • 料理甫毕 - Indicates that the preparations or care were just completed.
  • 举儿付之 - Means 'to hand over the child to someone.'
  • 告其叔 - Means 'to inform his uncle.'
  • 觅人舁产妇至寓 - Means 'to find someone to carry the pregnant woman to the residence.'
  • 一家团聚 - Means 'the family reunites.'
  • 招呼老媪，则已不见 - Describes calling out to the old woman, who then disappears.
  • 频年离乱 - Means 'years of turmoil or chaos.'
  • 举业已荒 - Means 'studies have been neglected.'
  • 不得不作揣摩计 - Means 'one must resort to speculative or preparatory measures.'
  • 明年可谢遣问字者 - Means 'next year, you can dismiss those who come to ask for instruction.'
  • 我虽贫，助汝脱粟十石 - Means 'although I am poor, I will help you with ten shi of coarse rice.'
  • 夫妇诸儿尚可不馁 - Means 'your wife, children, and others can still avoid hunger.'
  • 甚荷虀盐膏火 - Means 'greatly rely on the salt, vinegar, oil, and fire' (a metaphor for basic necessities).
  • 妇以十指襄之 - Means 'the wife uses her ten fingers to assist' (a metaphor for her hard work).
  • 若来秋复落 - Means 'if next autumn again fails' (referring to failing in an exam or endeavor).
  • 亦无颜见叔矣 - Means 'there would be no face to see the uncle anymore.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 司农 - Refers to the official responsible for agriculture or granaries in ancient China.
  • 妊 - Refers to pregnancy.
  • 白衣老媪 - Describes an elderly woman dressed in white, possibly a symbolic or supernatural figure.
  • 砺甲断脐 - Refers to the act of cutting the umbilical cord, which was often done with a sharp object in ancient times.
  • 裂裳作褓 - Refers to the practice of making swaddling clothes from torn clothing, a common practice in ancient times.
  • 频年离乱 - Refers to years of political or social turmoil, a common theme in Chinese history.
  • 举业 - Refers to the pursuit of scholarly or literary achievements, often associated with the imperial examination system.
  • 揣摩计 - Refers to speculative or preparatory measures, often taken in times of uncertainty.
  • 脱粟 - Refers to coarse rice, a basic food staple in ancient China.
  • 虀盐膏火 - Refers to basic necessities such as salt, vinegar, oil, and fire, often used metaphorically to describe essential resources.
  • 十指 - Refers to the ten fingers, used metaphorically to describe manual labor or hard work.
  • 问字者 - Refers to people who come to ask for instruction or teach, often associated with the imperial examination system.
  • 来秋复落 - Refers to failing in an exam or endeavor, a common concern for scholars in ancient China.
  • 无颜见叔 - Refers to the shame of failing to meet expectations, often associated with family honor.

Proper Nouns

  • 司农 - The official in charge of agriculture or granaries.
  • 皇朝定鼎 - Refers to the establishment of a new dynasty.
  • 丁亥 - A year in the Chinese zodiac, corresponding to the year of the Fire Rat.
  • 叔 - Refers to the uncle.
  • 侍御 - Refers to the scholar or official.
  • 夫人 - Refers to the wife.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 轮回之说 - The doctrine of rebirth, a Buddhist concept.
  • 说部记载颇多 - Many records in literary works, indicating the topic is well-documented.
  • 以余所闻 - According to what I have heard, introducing personal testimony.
  • 尤觉其信而可徵也 - Especially, I find it credible and verifiable, expressing a personal judgment on the reliability of the accounts.
  • 不知所从来 - Unknown origin, indicating the mysterious background of the person.
  • 以钝根求悟 - To seek enlightenment with a dull mind, suggesting a lack of innate spiritual insight.
  • 效二祖所为 - To imitate the actions of the Second Patriarch, referring to the practice of self-mutilation for spiritual purification.
  • 尚书祖母钱太夫人重之 - The grandmother of the Minister, Lady Qian, highly regarded him, indicating the person's status and the respect he received.
  • 供养朱家阁指松庵中 - To be cared for in the Zhujia Pavilion, Pine Hermitage, describing the place where the monk was hosted.
  • 时太翁农部家居 - At the time, the grandfather, Minister of Agriculture, was living at home, providing context for the setting.
  • 宅在秀野桥西 - The residence was located west of Xiu Ye Bridge, giving a specific location.
  • 母王恭人方妊 - The mother, Lady Wang, was pregnant, setting the scene for the birth.
  • 将就蓐 - About to give birth, indicating the imminent childbirth.
  • 饬家人呼乳医 - To instruct the family to call a midwife, describing the preparations for childbirth.
  • 恇扰彻夜 - Disturbed and anxious all night, conveying the emotional state of the father.
  • 于厅事隐几假寐 - To lean on a table in the hall and take a nap, describing the father's attempt to rest.
  • 曙色朦胧间 - In the dim light of dawn, setting the time of the event.
  • 恍惚见僧人入 - To see a monk entering in a daze, describing the vision.
  • 惊而醒 - To wake up in surprise, indicating the reaction to the vision.
  • 闻有关于门者则舟子与阍者争 - To hear a dispute between the boatman and the gatekeeper, describing the noise outside.
  • 方争论间，而庵中人来报 - While they were arguing, someone from the hermitage came to report, indicating the interruption.
  • 僧于半夜圆寂矣 - The monk passed away at midnight, describing the monk's death.
  • 遽返中堂 - To quickly return to the main hall, indicating the immediate action taken.
  • 婢已报夫人举雄 - The maid reported that the lady had given birth to a boy, confirming the birth.
  • 乃凿然知宿德再来也 - Thus, it became clear that the previous virtue had returned, expressing the realization that the monk's spirit had reincarnated.
  • 颖慧绝人 - Exceptionally intelligent and wise, describing the child's qualities.
  • 五岁时，农部以其多言，名之曰默 - At five years old, the Minister named him 'Mo' because he spoke too much, explaining the naming.
  • 一日，闻客呼少马字者 - One day, he heard a guest call for a name with the character '少马', introducing the naming incident.
  • 乃易之曰照，即以得天字焉 - So he changed his name to 'Zhao', which means 'to illuminate', and was given the courtesy name 'Tian', describing the final naming.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 轮回 - The Buddhist concept of rebirth, indicating a spiritual belief system.
  • 说部 - Literary works, often referring to historical or biographical texts.
  • 尚书 - A high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy, often associated with the Ministry of Rites or other important departments.
  • 钱中翰 - A historical figure, likely a scholar or official, known for his spiritual practices.
  • 天童寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Zhejiang Province, known for its spiritual significance.
  • 二祖 - The Second Patriarch of Chinese Buddhism, Huike, known for his practice of self-mutilation to seek enlightenment.
  • 朱家阁指松庵 - A specific hermitage or temple, likely a place of spiritual retreat.
  • 农部 - The Minister of Agriculture, a high-ranking official in the imperial government.
  • 秀野桥 - A specific bridge, likely a well-known landmark in the region.
  • 王恭人 - A title for a noblewoman, indicating a high social status.
  • 乳医 - A midwife, responsible for assisting in childbirth.
  • 圆寂 - To pass away in a state of enlightenment, a term used in Buddhism.
  • 宿德 - Previous virtue or merit, often used to describe a reincarnated soul.
  • 颖慧 - Exceptionally intelligent and wise, a term used to describe a child's intellectual capabilities.
  • 少马字 - A character that combines '少' (few) and '马' (horse), forming a name with a specific meaning.

Proper Nouns

  • 轮回 - The cycle of rebirth.
  • 说部 - Literary works.
  • 尚书 - A high-ranking official.
  • 钱中翰 - A historical figure.
  • 天童寺 - A famous Buddhist temple.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 十四入泮 - At the age of fourteen, he entered the school. *泮* refers to the school or academy.
  • 十七鄉舉 - At the age of seventeen, he was recommended by his local officials. *鄉舉* refers to the recommendation by local officials in the imperial examination system.
  • 十八捷南宮 - At the age of eighteen, he passed the palace examination. *捷* means to pass, and *南宮* refers to the palace examination.
  • 選庶常時 - When he was selected for the Hanlin Academy. *庶常* refers to the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution for scholars.
  • 奉欽點 - Was personally selected by the emperor. *欽點* means to be personally selected by the emperor.
  • 臣張照年幼，未嫻吏治 - Minister Zhang Zhao is young and inexperienced in administrative affairs. *未嫻吏治* means not yet proficient in government administration.
  • 教習，願盡中秘書 - I wish to be taught and to serve in the imperial library. *中秘書* refers to the imperial library.
  • 笑而頷之 - Smiled and nodded in agreement. *頷之* means to nod in approval.
  • 散館後 - After graduating from the Hanlin Academy. *散館* refers to the graduation from the Hanlin Academy.
  • 以檢討供奉內廷者十八年 - Served in the inner court as a censor for eighteen years. *檢討* refers to a censor, and *內廷* refers to the inner court.
  • 世宗即位後 - After Emperor Shizong ascended the throne. *世宗* refers to Emperor Shizong.
  • 廷臣中有通禪悅者否 - Are there any officials in the court who understand Zen Buddhism? *禪悅* refers to the joy of Zen meditation.
  • 視朕何人 - What do you see me as? *朕* is the first-person pronoun used by the emperor.
  • 干屎橛 - A piece of shit. This is a colloquial expression used by the monk.
  • 得放缺 - Was granted a vacant position. *放缺* means to be granted a vacant position.
  • 位至卿貳 - Reached the rank of a high official. *卿貳* refers to high-ranking officials.
  • 香火因緣 - Karmic connection through incense and fire. This refers to a spiritual connection.
  • 天瓶詩 - Poems of the Heavenly Vase. This is the title of Zhang Zhao's poetry collection.
  • 多採擇典題 - Frequently chose classic themes. *典題* refers to classic themes.
  • 多用字如生老病死、衣衾棺椁及夢幻泡影露電等 - Frequently used words like birth, aging, sickness, death, clothing, coffin, and the illusions of dreams and bubbles. *生老病死* refers to the four stages of life, *衣衾棺椁* refers to burial items, and *夢幻泡影露電* refers to the impermanence of life.
  • 不離梵筴 - Not far from Buddhist teachings. *梵筴* refers to Buddhist scriptures.
  • 讀者笑此真伽陵集也 - Readers laughed and said this is truly the collection of King Jambudipa. *伽陵集* refers to the collection of King Jambudipa, a Buddhist monk.
  • 扈從 - Accompanied the emperor on a trip. *扈從* means to accompany the emperor.
  • 山庄墜馬 - Fell off a horse at the imperial hunting grounds. *山庄* refers to the imperial hunting grounds.
  • 折右臂 - Broke his right arm. *折* means to break.
  • 得蒙古醫，療之而痊 - Was treated by a Mongol doctor and recovered. *蒙古醫* refers to a Mongol doctor.
  • 丁艱歸 - Returned home due to the death of a close relative. *丁艱* refers to the death of a close relative.
  • 卒於道 - Died on the road. *卒於道* means to die on the road.
  • 和尚誤矣 - The monk was mistaken. *和尚* refers to a monk.
  • 此福緣亦孽果也 - This good fortune is also a bad result. *福緣* refers to good fortune, and *孽果* refers to bad results.
  • 不如天童僧之能來去自如矣 - It is not as good as the ability of the monk Tiantong to come and go freely. *天童僧* refers to the monk Tiantong.
  • 杜陵苦熱詩 - Poems of Du Fu on the heat. *杜陵* refers to Du Fu, a famous poet.
  • 大醉後伏地 - After getting drunk, he fell to the ground. *伏地* means to fall to the ground.
  • 墨沈淋漓 - The ink was flowing profusely. *墨沈* refers to ink.
  • 得地居士 - The recluse of the earth. *得地居士* refers to a recluse.
  • 為稷堂先生備端陽貢 - Prepared a gift for Mr. Jitang for the Dragon Boat Festival. *稷堂先生* refers to Mr. Jitang, and *端陽貢* refers to a gift for the Dragon Boat Festival.
  • 收入內庭 - Collected into the inner court. *內庭* refers to the inner court.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 張照 - Zhang Zhao, a famous scholar and official during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 張文和 - Zhang Wenhe, likely a reference to Zhang Zhao's family or a title.
  • 世宗 - Emperor Shizong, the Yongzheng Emperor of the Qing Dynasty.
  • 禪悅 - Zen Buddhism, a form of Buddhism that emphasizes meditation and direct experience.
  • 香火因緣 - Karmic connection, a concept in Buddhism that refers to the spiritual connections between individuals.
  • 天瓶詩 - Zhang Zhao's poetry collection, which reflects his Buddhist influences.
  • 梵筴 - Buddhist scriptures, which Zhang Zhao frequently referenced in his poetry.
  • 伽陵集 - The collection of King Jambudipa, a Buddhist monk, which Zhang Zhao's poetry was compared to.
  • 蒙古醫 - A Mongol doctor, reflecting the cultural exchange between the Han and Mongol peoples during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 丁艱 - The death of a close relative, a significant event in Chinese culture that required mourning and returning home.
  • 天童僧 - The monk Tiantong, a famous Buddhist monk known for his spiritual freedom.
  • 杜陵 - Du Fu, a famous poet of the Tang Dynasty, known for his poems on various subjects.
  • 稷堂先生 - Mr. Jitang, likely a reference to a scholar or official.
  • 端陽貢 - A gift for the Dragon Boat Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 前生 - Refers to a past life or reincarnation, a concept from Buddhist philosophy.
  • 祈嗣 - Means 'to pray for offspring' or 'to seek descendants.'
  • 入定 - A meditative state in Buddhism, often translated as 'entering samadhi.'
  • 覘之 - Means 'to observe' or 'to inspect.'
  • 有省 - Means 'to realize' or 'to come to understand.'
  • 施舍 - Means 'to give alms' or 'to make charitable donations.'
  • 蓄资四十万 - Means 'accumulated wealth of forty thousand units' (likely referring to a currency or unit of wealth).
  • 四明 - Refers to a region in Zhejiang province, historically known for its scenic beauty.
  • 設齋飯 - Means 'to prepare a vegetarian meal' or 'to hold a vegetarian feast.'
  • 僧堂頭 - Refers to the head monk or abbot of a temple.
  • 曉眾 - Means 'to inform the congregation' or 'to announce to the assembly.'
  • 愕眙 - Means 'to stare in astonishment' or 'to look in surprise.'
  • 向錢一笑 - Means 'to smile at Mr. Qian.'
  • 肩有肉骈起 - Means 'shoulders with flesh growing together' or 'shoulders with flesh overlapping.'
  • 絕肖 - Means 'exactly like' or 'identical to.'
  • 初名鼎瑞，字寶汾 - Means 'initially named Dingrui, with the courtesy name Baofen.'
  • 更名后乃字葆汾 - Means 'after changing his name, his courtesy name became Baofen.'
  • 孝廉 - A scholar-official selected through the imperial examination system.
  • 中翰 - A title for a scholar-official, often translated as 'censor' or 'official in the Hanlin Academy.'
  • 詩號金門 - Means 'poet with the pseudonym Jinmen.'
  • 詞名湘瑟 - Means 'lyricist with the pseudonym Xiangse.'
  • 工艷體 - Means 'skilled in elegant or delicate style.'
  • 絕無蔬筍氣語 - Means 'completely devoid of the air of vegetables and bamboo shoots,' a metaphor for lacking rustic or simple qualities.
  • 奏銷獄起 - Refers to a legal case or scandal involving financial matters.
  • 镌秩家居 - Means 'to be demoted and live at home.'
  • 性好奕 - Means 'enjoys playing chess.'
  • 設楸枰 - Means 'to set up a chessboard.'
  • 天童山 - Refers to a mountain in Zhejiang, known for its Buddhist temples.
  • 空缄 - Means 'an empty letter' or 'a letter with no message.'
  • 示微疾 - Means 'showed slight illness' or 'became slightly ill.'
  • 作偈 - Means 'to compose a Buddhist verse.'
  • 示微疾，数日而逝 - Means 'showed slight illness and passed away after a few days.'
  • 其孙长泽 - Refers to his grandson Changze.
  • 棋入第二品 - Means 'chess player of the second rank' or 'chess player of high skill.'
  • 述之甚悉 - Means 'described it in great detail.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 前明 - Refers to the Ming dynasty.
  • 钱少马士贵 - A historical figure, likely a scholar-official.
  • 五十无子 - Indicates that he was fifty years old and had no children.
  • 祈嗣天童 - Refers to praying for descendants at Tian童 Mountain, a place known for its Buddhist practices.
  • 住山长老 - Refers to an elderly monk residing in the mountains.
  • 耆宿 - Refers to an elderly and respected person, often used for monks or scholars.
  • 入定 - A meditative state in Buddhism.
  • 覘之 - To observe or inspect, often used in the context of spiritual or mystical practices.
  • 有省 - To realize or come to understand, often used in the context of spiritual enlightenment.
  • 施舍 - To give alms or make charitable donations, a common practice in Buddhism.
  • 蓄资四十万 - Accumulated wealth of forty thousand units, likely referring to a currency or unit of wealth.
  • 四明 - A region in Zhejiang province, historically known for its scenic beauty.
  • 設齋飯 - To prepare a vegetarian meal or hold a vegetarian feast, a common practice in Buddhist temples.
  • 僧堂頭 - The head monk or abbot of a temple.
  • 曉眾 - To inform the congregation or announce to the assembly.
  • 愕眙 - To stare in astonishment or look in surprise.
  • 向錢一笑 - To smile at Mr. Qian, indicating a recognition or acknowledgment.
  • 肩有肉骈起 - Shoulders with flesh growing together or overlapping, a physical characteristic.
  • 絕肖 - Exactly like or identical to, indicating a strong resemblance.
  • 初名鼎瑞，字寶汾 - Initially named Dingrui, with the courtesy name Baofen.
  • 更名后乃字葆汾 - After changing his name, his courtesy name became Baofen.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蠢子数 - A pseudonym or title, likely referring to a person or author who claimed to be a follower or student of the famous scholar Sima Guang (康节先生). The term '蠢子' can be interpreted as 'a simple-minded person' or 'a naive scholar,' while '数' suggests a focus on numbers or calculations, possibly indicating a numerological or divinatory practice.
  • 汗牛充栋 - A classical idiom meaning 'so numerous that they cause oxen to sweat and fill houses,' used to describe an abundance of books or written works.
  • 合即掣一幅付之 - If the conditions align, a piece of paper (or a scroll) is drawn and given to the person. This likely refers to a divination result being presented in written form.
  • 始末胪列，真名称其实也 - The beginning and end of the divination are listed, and the true name or identity is revealed. This suggests that the divination provides a clear and detailed outcome.
  • 或骈或散，或文或俗 - The language used in the divination can be either parallel (骈) or free (散), formal (文) or colloquial (俗). This indicates that the divination results can vary in style and tone.
  • 若隐若明，若庄若谑 - The divination results can be vague or clear, serious or humorous. This suggests that the divination can be ambiguous and playful, depending on the context.
  • 狡狯殊甚 - The divination results are highly cunning or clever. This implies that the divination is not straightforward and may involve subtle or deceptive elements.
  • 由誊录起家 - Started from a lowly position as a copyist. This refers to someone who began their career by copying texts, which was a common occupation in ancient China.
  • 繇辞似科第而非科第 - The words used in the divination resemble those of a scholar-official but are not actually those of a scholar-official. This suggests that the divination mimics the style of scholarly writing but lacks the official status.
  • 微词嘲讽 - Subtle criticism or mockery. This indicates that the divination contains hidden or indirect criticism.
  • 殊见滑稽 - Particularly humorous or absurd. This suggests that the divination results are not only critical but also entertaining or absurd.
  • 初任金华佐，两字借点极巧 - Initially appointed as an assistant in Jinhua, the two characters used were extremely clever. This refers to a specific instance where the divination provided a clever solution or prediction.
  • 继而题长兴 - Then, the divination was used to predict the situation in Changxing. This suggests that the divination was applied to a different location or context.
  • 晋西防，保堪陛，擢福州 - Later, the person was promoted to positions in western Shanxi, the capital, and then to Fuzhou. This indicates a series of promotions in the person's career.
  • 以至被议捐复 - Eventually, the person was criticized and demoted. This suggests that the person faced some form of official censure or demotion.
  • 语皆暗合 - All the statements were indirectly correct. This implies that the divination results, though not explicitly stated, were still accurate in a subtle way.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 康节先生 - Refers to Sima Guang (1019–1086), a prominent statesman, historian, and scholar of the Song Dynasty. He is known for his work on the *Zizhi Tongjian* (Comprehensive Mirror to Aid in Government), a monumental historical text. The term '康节先生' is a respectful title given to him.
  • 晋省 - Refers to the province of Shanxi (山西), located in northern China. The term '晋' is an abbreviation for Shanxi.
  • 太原 - The capital city of Shanxi Province, located in northern China.
  • 袁方伯柏田 - Refers to a specific individual, likely a government official or scholar, whose name is 'Yuan Fangbo' and whose courtesy name or pseudonym is 'Baitian.' The term '方伯' is an ancient title for a high-ranking official, often used to refer to provincial governors.
  • 誊录 - A traditional occupation involving the copying of texts, often performed by low-ranking officials or scholars. This was a common way for people to enter the bureaucracy.
  • 科第 - Refers to the imperial examination system, which was the primary means of selecting government officials in China. '科' refers to the subject of the exam, and '第' refers to the rank or grade achieved.
  • 金华 - A city in Zhejiang Province, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 长兴 - A county in Zhejiang Province, near Hangzhou.
  • 晋西防 - Refers to a defensive position in western Shanxi, likely during a time of military conflict or tension.
  • 保堪陛 - Refers to a position in the capital city, likely a high-ranking official position.
  • 福州 - The capital city of Fujian Province, located in southeastern China.
  • 被议捐复 - Refers to a situation where a government official was criticized and demoted. '捐' can mean to donate or give up, and '复' means to restore or reinstate, but in this context, it implies a demotion or loss of position.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 蠢子数托名康节先生 - This phrase is ambiguous because it is unclear whether '蠢子数' is claiming to be a follower of Sima Guang or if it is a title given to someone who imitates his style. The phrase '托名' (claiming to be) adds to this ambiguity.
  • 其书汗牛充栋 - This phrase is a metaphorical expression that describes the abundance of books or written works. The literal meaning is difficult to translate directly into English, so it should be rendered in a way that conveys the idea of a vast collection of writings.
  • 其法先问父母生卒，及娶妻生子岁月 - This sentence is a list of specific questions that must be asked in the divination process. The structure is straightforward, but the cultural significance of these questions (related to birth, marriage, and death) should be preserved in the translation.
  • 合即掣一幅付之，始末胪列，真名称其实也 - This sentence describes the process of divination when the conditions align. The phrase '掣一幅付之' (draw a piece of paper and give it to the person) is a specific action that should be clearly explained in the translation.
  • 或骈或散，或文或俗，若隐若明，若庄若谑 - This sentence describes the varying styles and tones of the divination results. The parallel structure (骈 or 散, 文 or 俗) should be preserved in the translation to maintain the rhythm and balance of the original text.
  • 由誊录起家，繇辞似科第而非科第 - This sentence describes the career trajectory of a person who started as a copyist but later gained recognition through their writing. The contrast between '科第' (imperial examination) and '非科第' (not an official) should be clearly conveyed in the translation.
  • 微词嘲讽，殊见滑稽 - This sentence describes the subtle criticism and humor found in the divination results. The phrase '殊见滑稽' (particularly humorous) should be translated in a way that captures both the humor and the absurdity of the situation.
  • 初任金华佐，两字借点极巧 - This sentence describes a specific instance where the divination provided a clever solution. The phrase '两字借点极巧' (two characters used in a very clever way) should be translated in a way that highlights the ingenuity of the divination.
  • 继而题长兴 - This sentence describes the application of the divination to a different location. The phrase '继而' (then) indicates a sequence of events, which should be preserved in the translation.
  • 晋西防，保堪陛，擢福州 - This sentence describes a series of promotions in the person's career. The phrase '擢' (promote) should be translated in a way that conveys the upward trajectory of the person's career.
  • 以至被议捐复，语皆暗合 - This sentence describes the person's eventual demotion and the fact that the divination results were still accurate in a subtle way. The phrase '语皆暗合' (all the statements were indirectly correct) should be translated in a way that captures the subtlety of the divination results.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 長君厚堂竟前卒 - 長君指高級官員或貴族，厚堂可能是稱號或綽號，竟前卒意為「早逝」或「英年早逝」。
  • 亦驗 - 亦意為「也」或「同樣」，驗可指「有效」或「驗證」。
  • 一為完顏制軍魁倫 - 完顏是女真族的常見姓氏，制軍是軍事頭銜，魁倫是個人名。
  • 時制闽浙 - 時制意為「當時控制」，闽浙指福建和浙江兩省。
  • 以閱兵駐武林 - 以閱兵意為「為了檢閱部隊」，駐武林意為「駐紮在武林（杭州）」。
  • 萼樓張觀察郵寄以示僚屬 - 萼樓可能是地名或綽號，張觀察是頭銜和名字，郵寄意為「通過郵件寄送」，以示僚屬意為「展示給下屬」。
  • 前半節目 - 前半節目意為「計劃的第一部分」或「最初的計劃」。
  • 眾均未悉 - 眾均未悉意為「大家都沒有意識到」或「沒有人知道」。
  • 制軍則曰甚驗 - 制軍與完顏制軍魁倫相同，則曰甚驗意為「然後說非常有效」。
  • 眾皆貢諛謂入閣且襲爵也 - 眾皆貢諛意為「大家都奉承」，謂意為「說」，入閣意為「進入內閣」，襲爵意為「繼承爵位」。
  • 魁亦居之不疑 - 魁與完顏制軍魁倫相同，居之不疑意為「接受而不懷疑」。
  • 蓋爾時以參伍浦，眷甚隆 - 蓋爾時意為「當時」，參伍浦可能是地名或頭銜，眷甚隆意為「深受寵愛」。
  • 拜相封公，亦意中事 - 拜相意為「成為宰相」，封公意為「被封為公爵」，亦意中事意為「也是預期的事情」。
  • 但雜以生死字樣，疑之 - 但意為「但是」，雜以意為「混合著」，生死意為「生死」，字樣意為「文字」，疑之意為「懷疑」。
  • 大約極品後，即考終耳 - 大約意為「大約」，極品意為「最高級」，即意為「然後」，考終意為「安詳去世」，耳是語氣詞，意為「只」或「僅」。
  • 其後仍有恩膏優渥，錫予頻頻等語 - 其後意為「後來」，仍有意為「仍然有」，恩膏優渥意為「恩惠和優厚待遇」，錫予意為「賜予」，頻頻意為「頻繁」。
  • 益信其吉 - 益信意為「更加相信」，其吉意為「那是好事」。
  • 豈知為賜帛讖 - 豈知意為「誰知道」，為意為「是」，賜帛意為「賜予絲綢」，讖意為「預言」或「徵兆」。
  • 異哉 - 異哉是驚歎詞，意為「奇怪」或「不尋常」。
  • 生死關頭，乃嚴旨中語 - 生死關頭意為「生死攸關」，乃意為「是」，嚴旨意為「嚴格的詔書」，中語意為「其中的話」。
  • 尤奇 - 尤意為「尤其」，奇意為「奇怪」或「不尋常」。
  • 死後仁宗念其前功 - 死後意為「去世後」，仁宗是年號，念意為「記得」，其前功意為「他以前的功績」。
  • 恤還沒產 - 恤意為「安慰」或「提供救助」，沒產意為「孤兒」。
  • 且錄其子 - 且意為「並且」，錄意為「記錄」或「任命」，其子意為「他的兒子」。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 石湖之萬笏朝天 - A poetic expression describing the landscape of Shihu (Stone Lake), where the scenery resembles countless tablets (笏) pointing to the sky. This is a metaphorical way of describing the natural beauty and grandeur of the area.
  • 與黃山之千株臨蕩 - Another metaphorical expression comparing the landscape of Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) to thousands of trees leaning over a vast expanse (蕩). This emphasizes the scale and majesty of the scenery.
  • 均黃石 - Both landscapes are described as being composed of yellowish rocks (黄石), which is a key characteristic of the terrain.
  • 質純 - This phrase means 'pure in quality,' referring to the unadulterated nature of the rocks.
  • 仙凡之別 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'the difference between the divine and the mundane,' highlighting the otherworldly quality of the scenery compared to ordinary landscapes.
  • 戀戀幾不成行 - A poetic expression meaning 'so enamored that one hesitates to leave,' describing the narrator's deep appreciation of the scenery.
  • 馬上猶回首數四 - This phrase means 'even on horseback, one turns back several times,' emphasizing the narrator's reluctance to depart.
  • 後每逢迎送，輒留一宿 - This means 'later, whenever I met or saw off guests, I would always stay for a night,' indicating a habit of lingering due to the beauty of the place.
  • 然終未若黃平之飛云洞 - This phrase means 'yet it was never like the Feiyun Cave in Huangping,' expressing a preference for another location.
  • 至今猶夢寐遇之也 - This means 'even now, I still dream of encountering it,' suggesting a lasting impression.
  • 言山水之勝，曰雄曰奇，曰秀曰麗 - This is a list of adjectives describing the beauty of mountains and rivers, meaning 'when speaking of the beauty of mountains and rivers, one says they are majestic, strange, beautiful, and elegant.'
  • 飛云洞咸背焉 - This means 'the Feiyun Cave is different from all of these,' indicating that it does not fit into the usual categories of beauty.
  • 將至數里，見千尋挺立 - This describes the approach to the cave, with '千尋' meaning 'thousands of feet high,' emphasizing the towering cliffs.
  • 古杉參天 - This means 'ancient pines reaching the sky,' describing the tall trees surrounding the area.
  • 無所謂洞也 - This phrase means 'there is no such thing as a cave,' indicating that the entrance is not obvious.
  • 尋之在絕戶之下 - This means 'searching for it, it is found below a sheer cliff,' describing the location of the cave.
  • 翳荟交蔽 - This means 'overgrown with dense vegetation,' describing the lush surroundings.
  • 水簾一桁 - This means 'a waterfall like a curtain,' describing the waterfall that blocks the entrance.
  • 由其側螺旋而入 - This means 'entering by spiraling around the side,' describing the path into the cave.
  • 已倏在水簾内矣 - This means 'one suddenly finds oneself inside the waterfall,' describing the unexpected experience of entering the cave.
  • 蓋沸泉據山顶，奔騰而下 - This means 'the hot spring is at the top of the mountain, rushing down,' describing the source of the waterfall.
  • 至半山，石忽横裂一罅 - This means 'reaching halfway up the mountain, the rock suddenly splits open,' describing the formation of the waterfall.
  • 瀑布千仞，自天而下 - This means 'a waterfall thousands of feet high, falling from the sky,' emphasizing the height and grandeur of the waterfall.
  • 冬夏不绝 - This means 'never ceasing, winter or summer,' indicating the constant flow of the waterfall.
  • 洞中石如朵朵碧云，乘风欲去 - This means 'the rocks inside the cave resemble blooming blue clouds, as if they are about to be carried away by the wind,' describing the ethereal appearance of the cave.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 石湖 - Refers to Shihu (Stone Lake), a scenic area in China known for its natural beauty.
  • 黃山 - Refers to Huangshan (Yellow Mountain), one of the most famous mountain ranges in China, renowned for its stunning landscapes.
  • 飛云洞 - Refers to Feiyun Cave, a specific cave in Huangping, known for its unique and mystical features.
  • 山水之勝 - Refers to the beauty of mountains and rivers, a common theme in Chinese literature and art.
  • 仙凡之別 - Refers to the distinction between the divine and the mundane, a recurring theme in Chinese philosophy and literature.

  • 黃平 - Huangping

  • 予谓冷泉以秀胜，虹桥以丽胜，丹崖以雄胜，碧鸡以奇胜，飞云兼而有之 - I say that Cold Spring is beautiful, Rainbow Bridge is splendid, Red Cliff is majestic, Blue Rooster is unique, and Flying Cloud combines all these qualities; compares different scenic spots.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 苗俗 - Refers to the customs or practices of the Miao people, a term used to describe certain ethnic groups in southern China.
  • 猺獞狼猡，字皆从犬 - A classical description of the Miao people, where their names all contain the character for 'dog,' suggesting their position between humans and animals.
  • 明其在人兽之介也 - A statement explaining that the Miao people are in a middle ground between humans and animals.
  • 杀人不闻以牛代 - A statement indicating that in other regions, killing someone is not replaced by a cow.
  • 若迤东三府，则此语殊未确矣 - A statement suggesting that in the three eastern prefectures, this statement is not entirely accurate.
  • 昭通东川开化，俗狞且蠢 - Describes the customs of Zhaotong, Dongchuan, and Kaihua as cruel and stupid.
  • 命案最多，皆不办，明目张胆 - States that there are many murder cases, all of which are not handled, and are committed openly.
  • 牛亦视家之贫富，富者至千，贫者数百，不尽牛也 - The number of cows depends on the family's wealth, ranging from a thousand for the rich to several hundred for the poor.
  • 首曰牛头，末曰牛尾，中曰牛腰，必以牛，外则束缣牛也，炊甑牛也，甚至双履亦牛也，罄其家之所有而已 - The compensation can be anything from the head of a cow to the tail, the middle part, or even non-cow items like bundles of silk, cooking pots, or even shoes, using up everything the family has.
  • 当事者或绳之以法，则大哗，谓此伧非人情，我家不幸死于斗，孤儿寡妇，赖牛以活，故曰偿 - If officials try to enforce the law, there is great uproar, claiming that this practice is not humane, and that the families of the deceased rely on the cow to survive, hence it is called compensation.
  • 若诛其人，虽菹醢之，于死者何益，于生者更何益，安所谓偿？ - If the perpetrator is punished, even if they are executed, it would not benefit the dead or the living, so what is the point of compensation?
  • 无已，分其牛为贽，以间执其口 - As a compromise, the cow is divided as a gift to avoid further conflict.
  • 一牛折金四，或十或八，则分防之公费也，上游亦无从禁之 - One cow is worth four, ten, or eight units of currency, which is used as public funds, and the higher authorities cannot stop it.
  • 不然，凶手固无辞，尸亲必违抗，案终不了矣 - Otherwise, the murderer would have no defense, and the family of the victim would resist, leaving the case unresolved.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苗俗 - Refers to the customs of the Miao people, a term used to describe certain ethnic groups in southern China.
  • 黄陶菴先生 - Huang Tao'an, a scholar who commented on the text 'The People All Thought the King Was Loving.'
  • 百姓皆以王为爱也 - A text that suggests the people thought the king was loving, but in this context, it is used to highlight the contrast with the Miao custom.
  • 昭通东川开化 - Refers to the regions of Zhaotong, Dongchuan, and Kaihua in Yunnan Province.
  • 牛 - Cows are used as a form of compensation in the Miao custom, reflecting the economic and social practices of the time.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 猺獞狼猡，字皆从犬，明其在人兽之介也 - This sentence describes the Miao people as being between humans and animals, using a metaphorical description of their names.
  • 杀人不闻以牛代，若迤东三府，则此语殊未确矣 - This sentence contrasts the general practice of not using cows as compensation with the specific practice in the three eastern prefectures.
  • 命案最多，皆不办，明目张胆 - This sentence describes the prevalence and open nature of murder cases in the region.
  • 牛亦视家之贫富，富者至千，贫者数百，不尽牛也 - This sentence explains the variability in the number of cows based on the family's wealth.
  • 首曰牛头，末曰牛尾，中曰牛腰，必以牛，外则束缣牛也，炊甑牛也，甚至双履亦牛也，罄其家之所有而已 - This sentence lists the various forms of compensation, including non-cow items.
  • 当事者或绳之以法，则大哗，谓此伧非人情，我家不幸死于斗，孤儿寡妇，赖牛以活，故曰偿 - This sentence describes the reaction of the community when officials try to enforce the law.
  • 若诛其人，虽菹醢之，于死者何益，于生者更何益，安所谓偿？ - This sentence argues against the effectiveness of legal punishment in this context.
  • 无已，分其牛为贽，以间执其口 - This sentence describes the compromise reached to avoid conflict.
  • 一牛折金四，或十或八，则分防之公费也，上游亦无从禁之 - This sentence explains the value of the cow and the use of the funds.
  • 不然，凶手固无辞，尸亲必违抗，案终不了矣 - This sentence concludes that without this compromise, the case would remain unresolved.

Proper Nouns

  • 百姓皆以王为爱也 - A text that suggests the people thought the king was loving.
  • 昭通东川开化 - The regions of Zhaotong, Dongchuan, and Kaihua in Yunnan Province.

  • 因碎其碣，焚其案 - Means 'therefore, they smashed their steles and burned their cases,' a symbolic act of resolving the dispute.

  • 执梃为幕宾盟长 - Literally 'holding a cudgel as the chief of the inner circle of advisors'; a metaphorical way of saying he was the leading advisor or strategist.
  • 馆于近花圃 - Means 'resided in a nearby garden,' suggesting a pleasant or prestigious location.
  • 园林戏台咸备 - Means 'all the garden and theater facilities were complete,' indicating a well-equipped residence.
  • 督及抚尚可折简招 - Means 'the governor and the provincial commissioner could still summon him with a simple letter,' indicating his high status.
  • 道府以下有君前、无士前也 - Means 'below the level of the governor, there were those who addressed him as 'lord' but none as 'scholar,' indicating his elevated position.
  • 左鞹贮刑名，右鞹藏钱谷 - Means 'the left bag held legal matters, and the right bag held financial matters,' indicating his dual expertise.
  • 视王二先生点定，即遣奴呈两府施行 - Means 'when Wang Er reviewed and approved, the servants would present the decisions to the two offices for implementation,' indicating his authority.
  • 一缺出，官须两司议详、两院商定 - Means 'when a vacancy occurred, officials had to consult with two departments and two courts,' indicating a complex decision-making process.
  • 幕则立翁片言而决 - Means 'the chief advisor, however, would make a decision with just a few words,' indicating his decisive authority.
  • 当局者不敢参一词 - Means 'those in charge dared not add a single word,' indicating the respect and deference he commanded.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王立人 - The subject of the passage, a man from Shanyin, Zhejiang, known for his expertise in drafting official documents and legal matters.
  • 越之山阴 - Refers to Shanyin, a county in Zhejiang Province, historically known for its cultural and political significance.
  • 摺奏 - A type of official document or memorial used in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 刑名 - Refers to criminal law and legal procedures.
  • 钱谷 - Refers to financial matters, including taxation and treasury management.
  • 幕宾盟长 - Refers to the chief advisor or strategist in the inner circle of an official.
  • 督及抚 - Refers to the governor and the provincial commissioner, high-ranking officials in the provincial administration.
  • 道府 - Refers to the governor of a prefecture, a lower-ranking official.
  • 承宣 - Refers to the official in charge of communications and correspondence.
  • 提刑 - Refers to the official in charge of judicial affairs.
  • 首府 - Refers to the head of the local administration.
  • 缟紵 - Refers to a close friendship or alliance, often used in political contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 德清许君祖京 - Refers to Xu Zujing from Deqing, the official in charge of communications.
  • 江夏贺君长庚 - Refers to He Changgeng from Jiangxia, the official in charge of judicial affairs.
  • 武林庄君肇奎 - Refers to Zhuang Zaoqi from Wulin, the head of the local administration.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 滇省修脯最优 - 滇省 refers to Yunnan Province, and 修脯 is an archaic term for salary or stipend. The phrase means that salaries in Yunnan are the highest.
  • 即至薄者亦六百金 - 即至薄者 means 'even the lowest,' and 六百金 refers to six hundred taels of silver, a significant amount in the context of the time.
  • 繁缺倍之 - 繁缺 refers to busy or important positions, and 倍之 means 'twice as much.' This phrase indicates that salaries for such positions are double the standard.
  • 皆其门下士 - 门下士 refers to disciples or followers of a master.
  • 然亦甚公 - 然亦甚公 means 'but it is still very fair.'
  • 以技之长短分高下 - 以技之长短 means 'based on the length or quality of their skills,' and 分高下 means 'to rank them in order.'
  • 不少轩轾也 - 轩轾 refers to the back and front of a carriage, used metaphorically to mean 'favoritism' or 'bias.' This phrase means that there is no bias in the ranking.
  • 各府厅州县衙参大府后 - 各府厅州县 refers to various administrative levels, and 参大府后 means 'after visiting the provincial governor.'
  • 午必麕集 - 麕集 means 'to gather in large numbers.'
  • 谒贵者于斯 - 谒贵者 means 'to visit the wealthy or influential,' and 于斯 means 'here.'
  • 审案者亦于斯 - 审案者 means 'those who judge cases,' and 亦于斯 means 'also here.'
  • 娱戏者尤必于斯 - 娱戏者 means 'those who entertain,' and 尤必于斯 means 'especially here.'
  • 一厅则敲扑喧哗 - 敲扑 refers to the sound of wooden blocks or gongs, and 喧哗 means 'noisy.'
  • 一厅则笙歌婀娜 - 笙歌 refers to music and singing, and 婀娜 means 'graceful.'
  • 不相闻，不相混 - 不相闻 means 'not hearing each other,' and 不相混 means 'not mixing with each other.'
  • 夜必设筵 - 夜必设筵 means 'a banquet is set up every night.'
  • 器物多创造 - 器物 refers to utensils or objects, and 多创造 means 'many are newly created.'
  • 如大方机、阔茶几 - 大方机 and 阔茶几 refer to large tables and wide tea tables, respectively.
  • 人占一机一几 - 人占一机一几 means 'each person has one table and one tea table.'
  • 进食单以笔点之 - 进食单 refers to a menu, and 以笔点之 means 'marked with a pen.'
  • 一壶一簋，不并案 - 一壶一簋 refers to one pot and one bowl, and 不并案 means 'not placed on the same table.'
  • 或欲遍尝，或不兼味 - 或欲遍尝 means 'some want to try everything,' and 或不兼味 means 'some do not want to eat multiple dishes.'
  • 惟其便 - 惟其便 means 'as they please.'
  • 绍酒大尊价纹银十二两 - 绍酒 refers to Shaoxing wine, 大尊 means 'large jar,' and 纹银十二两 means 'twelve taels of silver.'
  • 夜必罄一尊 - 夜必罄一尊 means 'a large jar of wine is consumed every night.'
  • 同乡之赋闲者，悉馆谷之 - 同乡之赋闲者 refers to idle fellow townspeople, and 悉馆谷之 means 'all are provided for.'
  • 故王二先生，大府有投赠，司道有缟紵，府厅以下有进奉 - 王二先生 refers to Mr. Wang Er, 大府 means 'provincial governor,' 司道 refers to officials, 缟紵 refers to silk and cloth, and 府厅以下有进奉 means 'officials below the level of prefecture and county make offerings.'
  • 而终不足供其挥霍 - 终不足供其挥霍 means 'still not enough to satisfy his extravagance.'
  • 于是谋于当事，总办各省铜运 - 于是谋于当事 means 'he then consulted with the relevant authorities,' and 总办各省铜运 means 'to oversee the copper transportation across provinces.'
  • 除京运八起，及粤省以盐交易外 - 除京运八起 means 'except for the eight shipments to Beijing,' and 及粤省以盐交易外 means 'and the trade of salt with Guangdong Province.'
  • 期余各省以铜本交藩库，即以运本交王寓 - 期余各省以铜本交藩库 means 'the copper from the remaining provinces is to be delivered to the provincial treasury,' and 即以运本交王寓 means 'and the transportation costs are to be paid to Mr. Wang's residence.'
  • 届期则于百色兑铜 - 届期 means 'at the appointed time,' and 于百色兑铜 means 'in Baise, the copper is exchanged.'
  • 既速且逸 - 既速且逸 means 'both fast and easy.'
  • 运员以恬以娱 - 运员 refers to transport officials, 以恬 means 'to be at ease,' and 以娱 means 'to be entertained.'
  • 省之近者，且可暂假归省 - 省之近者 means 'those close to the province,' and 且可暂假归省 means 'can also temporarily take leave to return home.'

  • 门下士 - Refers to disciples or followers of a master, indicating a hierarchical relationship.

  • 司道 - Refers to officials, likely including both civil and military officers.
  • 府厅以下 - Refers to officials below the level of prefecture and county, indicating a broad range of administrative positions.
  • 馆谷 - Refers to providing food and lodging, a common practice in traditional Chinese society.
  • 铜运 - Refers to the transportation of copper, a valuable resource used for coinage and other purposes.
  • 藩库 - Refers to the provincial treasury, a central financial institution.
  • 百色 - Refers to Baise, a city in Guangxi Province, known for its role in copper trade.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一言不合，即可褰裳 - A hyperbolic expression meaning 'if there is even a slight disagreement, he can immediately leave,' emphasizing his quick temper.
  • 脯修以十日为断 - Means that he would only stay for ten days, indicating a short-term engagement.
  • 不过支 - Suggests that he was not staying longer than necessary, implying a lack of commitment.
  • 居停 - Refers to the host or the person who is providing accommodation.
  • 处分隔阂 - Means 'to handle or resolve conflicts or misunderstandings.'
  • 足下官职岂与生俱来耶 - A rhetorical question meaning 'Is your official position something you were born with?' It implies that the position was not inherent but rather granted by others.
  • 傥来之物 - Refers to something that has come unexpectedly or by chance, often used to describe wealth or power.
  • 奈何以民命徇之 - Means 'how can you sacrifice the lives of the people for such things?' It criticizes the misuse of power.
  • 上官或有偏倚 - Means 'if the superior officer is biased.'
  • 再三顶覆 - Means 'to repeatedly argue or plead,' indicating persistence in trying to correct a mistake.
  • 得申其意乃已 - Means 'only after his intentions have been fully expressed,' suggesting a thorough and determined effort.
  • 良有司 - Refers to a competent official.
  • 明大府 - Refers to a high-ranking official or a prominent government body.
  • 依然到处逢迎 - Means 'still able to find support and approval wherever he goes,' indicating that despite his strictness, he is well-regarded.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 竹垞先生 - Gu Yanwu, a renowned scholar and historian, known for his critical views on the Qing dynasty and his contributions to classical studies.
  • 滇 - Yunnan, a province in southwestern China, which was a frontier region during the Ming and Qing dynasties.
  • 幕 - Refers to the practice of serving as a retainer or advisor, often in military or administrative capacities.
  • 官职 - Refers to official positions, which were highly valued in traditional Chinese society.
  • 民命 - Refers to the lives of the common people, a central concern in Confucian ethics.
  • 上官 - Refers to superiors or higher-ranking officials, a key figure in the hierarchical structure of traditional Chinese governance.
  • 有司 - Refers to government officials, often used in a general sense to denote the bureaucracy.
  • 大府 - Refers to high-ranking officials or important government bodies, such as the Ministry of Rites or the Imperial Court.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 饭后，僮向某小语 - After dinner, the servant whispered something to someone.
  • 有日行件，须了之即来 - There is a matter that needs to be handled; I will return immediately after taking care of it.
  • 遂趋出 - He then hurriedly left.
  • 须臾，归局复博 - After a short while, he returned to the gaming house to resume gambling.
  • 至夜分，主戚戚，某怡怡，诸友皆惘惘 - By midnight, the host was anxious, the person was cheerful, and the friends were all confused.
  • 忽闻外间欢呼驰报曰："囚已尽获矣！" - Suddenly, they heard shouts from outside announcing, 'The prisoners have all been captured!'
  • 详稿出诸袖中，略云生苗反狱，逻者觉之，丞乃亲率丁壮，囚尚抗拒，营弁亦来，斗而检之 - He took out a detailed report from his sleeve, which roughly stated that the rebels had escaped from prison, the guards discovered them, the magistrate personally led the militia, the prisoners still resisted, and the military officers also came, and they fought and searched for them.
  • 然苗甚黠，颇多徒党，且擒时中有受伤将毙者，援边防例，即正典刑，防其逸或死，得逃显戮也 - However, the rebels were very cunning, had many accomplices, and some were injured and close to death when captured. According to border defense regulations, they were immediately executed to prevent them from escaping or dying, thus avoiding public execution.
  • 疏防及擅刑，均开参请议，盖去者不可追，别获七苗，李代桃僵也 - The negligence in defense and the unauthorized execution were both reported for discussion, as the escapees could not be pursued, and seven other rebels were captured instead, a case of 'substituting the plums for the peaches.'
  • 丞不惟无罪，且以能书功，斯真妙手回斡，能排难者 - The magistrate not only escaped blame but also received praise for his writing skills, truly a masterful hand that could resolve difficulties.
  • 但生苗逢人即杀，有可死之道，而雉纲鸿雁，此心何以自安哉 - However, the rebels killed everyone they encountered, and there was a way for them to die. But how can one's heart be at peace knowing that innocent people like caged chickens and wild geese were killed?
  • 未识秋芳闻之，又当如何也 - It is unclear how Autumn Fragrance would react upon hearing this.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 群苗聚而问 - The Miao people gathered and asked.
  • 皇帝召汝何事 - What business does the Emperor have with you? Here, '皇帝' is used to address local officials or leaders, not the actual emperor.
  • 苗人谓官无大小文武，皆曰皇帝 - The Miao people call all officials, regardless of rank or role, 'Emperor.' This reflects the Miao people's perception of authority.
  • 谓隶则曰官，书吏幕宾曰军师，至尊乃曰京师老皇帝 - They call subordinates 'officials,' scribes and attendants 'military advisors,' and the supreme leader 'the old emperor of the capital.' This shows the Miao's hierarchical understanding of authority.
  • 然畏官畏军师，皇帝不畏，老皇帝更不畏也 - However, they fear officials and military advisors, but not the Emperor, and even less so the old emperor of the capital. This highlights the Miao's fear of local authorities but not the central government.
  • 拥头人围巡检署索结，不可，杀而夺之，诸寨应之皆起，夫苗已反矣，索结何为？亦苗已反矣，藏结又何用？ - They surrounded the leader and the inspection office, demanding to settle the issue. When refused, they killed and took it by force. All the villages rose up, and the Miao had already rebelled. What is the point of settling the issue now? Even if they had not rebelled, what use would hiding the settlement be? This describes the escalation of the conflict.
  • 苗之为苗噩噩耳，施与苗如一邱之貉。何居？施得祸最惨，尽室并命，而因由如此。 - The Miao are just as simple-minded as they seem. The Han and the Miao are like foxes from the same den. Why? The Han suffered the most in the end, with their entire families being exterminated, all because of this. This reflects the author's view of the Miao and the Han as similar in their actions.
  • 易云：勿过防之，从或戕之。哀哉！然苗疆终止西南一隅，施君以身徇之，犹可自解，若戴如煌之墨而愎，乃至流毒天下矣。 - The *I Ching* says, 'Do not overprotect, for it may lead to harm.' Alas! The Miao region is confined to the southwestern corner, and the Han official sacrificed himself for them, which can still be justified. But if someone like Dai Ruhuang, who is stubborn and arrogant, were to follow, it could spread disaster throughout the land. This is a reflection on the consequences of the conflict.
  • 教匪狱起，通饬州县严查，蜀中素多邪教，达州牧戴如煌者，无材而嗜利，任人牵鼻。 - The case of the 'heretical bandits' arose, and the governor-general ordered all prefectures and counties to conduct strict investigations. In Sichuan, there are many heretical sects. Dai Ruhuang, the governor of Dazhou, was incompetent and greedy, allowing others to manipulate him. This describes the political and social context of the time.

  • 教匪 - Refers to 'heretical bandits,' a term used to describe rebels or insurgents who were seen as heretical or rebellious.
  • 蜀中 - Refers to Sichuan province, historically known for its diverse cultural and religious practices.

  • 戴如煌 - Refers to Dai Ruhuang, a historical figure mentioned in the text as a governor who was incompetent and greedy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 英公驰赴达州 - Refers to a noble or official named Ying rushing to Dazhou.
  • 括其兵 - Means to gather the troops.
  • 辅以壮役三百 - Means to support with 300 strong laborers.
  • 俾杨将之 - Means to appoint Yang as the leader.
  • 檄一如偕 - Refers to an official document or order, likely from a previous context.
  • 时贼已屯于金梁山 - Means the bandits had already camped on Jinliang Mountain.
  • 王役扼之太平寨 - Means Commander Wang blocked them at Taiping Fort.
  • 英至，适贼以百人来夺隘 - Means when Ying arrived, the bandits happened to attack the pass with 100 men.
  • 以兵壮敌之，杀数十人 - Means to fight them with the troops and kill dozens of people.
  • 英心易之，议进剿 - Means Ying thought it easy and discussed an advance to attack.
  • 一如止之，谓贼势不弱，非可轻举 - Means Yi Ru stopped him, saying that the bandits' strength was not weak and should not be underestimated.
  • 请拨兵二千，给饷十万，募乡勇益之，庶可一举而定 - Means to request the dispatch of 2,000 soldiers, provide 100,000 units of food, recruit local militias to strengthen the force, and hopefully settle the matter in one go.
  • 英公曰："姚君计诚万全，然苗事方亟，何处抽兵，十万饷作何开销，某将去之员，不足肩其任也。" - Means Ying said, "Yi Ru's plan is indeed perfect, but the Miao affair is urgent, where to draw soldiers from, how to spend 100,000 units of food, and I, who am leaving, am not capable of shouldering this responsibility."
  • 卒讨之 - Means to finally attack them.
  • 不转瞬间，已滋漫难图矣 - Means in no time, it had already spread and become difficult to handle.
  • 由是达副戎与陆令君以二千烬，继而珠都统施总戎以万余人溃 - Means Deputy General Da and County Magistrate Lu with 2,000 men were defeated, and subsequently, General Zhu and General Shi with over 10,000 men were defeated.
  • 而徐天德、王三槐等并起，祸延七省，萌蘖实惟戴之墨且愎酿之 - Means Xu Tiande, Wang Sanhuai, and others rose up, the disaster spread to seven provinces, and the root cause was actually Wei Dai's stubbornness and arrogance.
  • 洪编修稚存尤痛心疾首，至欲籍其家而戮其尸 - Means Hong Zicun was especially heartbroken and furious, even wanting to confiscate his family's property and kill his body.
  • 岂刻论哉 - Means how could this be an extreme statement?
  • 王役终为乱民所醢，亦一快事 - Means Commander Wang was ultimately killed by the rebels, which is also a good thing.
  • 戴牧得保首领，不克如汉州之剖腹而实以钱也倖矣 - Means Wei Dai managed to keep his head, but he was not as lucky as the man in Hanshou who had his abdomen cut open and filled with coins.
  • 但未与王白首同归，冥冥之中负此良役 - Means but he did not end up with Wang in old age, and in the afterlife, he bears this good servant.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 達州教匪 - Refers to rebels or bandits in Dazhou; '教匪' can mean 'religious rebels' or 'bandits'.
  • 以一隶肇之 - '肇' means 'to initiate' or 'to start'; '以一隶' means 'with one person' (likely a subordinate or follower).
  • 以一隶擒之 - '擒' means 'to capture' or 'to seize'.
  • 洵若辈亦未尝无人耳 - '洵' is an adverb meaning 'indeed' or 'certainly'; '若辈' refers to 'these people' or 'such individuals'; '亦未尝无人' means 'also not without someone' or 'also not without a person'.
  • 屡试不第 - '屡试' means 'many times tried' or 'repeatedly failed'; '不第' means 'failed to pass' or 'did not succeed'.
  • 萌逆志 - '萌' means 'to sprout' or 'to arise'; '逆志' means 'rebellious intentions'.
  • 结盟歃血 - '结盟' means 'to form an alliance'; '歃血' is a ritual of drinking blood to swear an oath.
  • 拟于明岁元旦起事 - '拟' means 'to plan' or 'to intend'; '明岁' means 'next year'; '元旦' means 'New Year's Day'; '起事' means 'to start a rebellion' or 'to rise up'.
  • 乘会府拜牌 - '乘' means 'to take advantage of'; '会府' refers to 'the governor's office' or 'the provincial government'; '拜牌' means 'to present a tablet' or 'to perform a ritual'.
  • 聚而歼之 - '聚' means 'to gather'; '歼' means 'to annihilate' or 'to destroy'.
  • 能戕某官者，即以其官官之 - '戕' means 'to kill' or 'to harm'; '即以其官官之' means 'will be given the position of that official'.
  • 成都府役柳姓 - '成都府' refers to 'the governor of Chengdu'; '役' means 'servant' or 'subordinate'; '柳姓' means 'a man surnamed Liu'.
  • 素能事 - '素' means 'usually' or 'habitually'; '能事' means 'capable of handling affairs' or 'skilled in business'.
  • 侦知其谋 - '侦' means 'to spy' or 'to investigate'; '知' means 'to know'; '其谋' means 'their plan'.
  • 苦无确据 - '苦' means 'hardly' or 'barely'; '无确据' means 'without solid evidence'.
  • 诈入其党 - '诈' means 'to pretend' or 'to feign'; '入' means 'to enter'; '其党' means 'their group' or 'their gang'.
  • 尽得名籍 - '尽' means 'completely' or 'thoroughly'; '得' means 'to obtain'; '名籍' means 'list of names' or 'register'.
  • 时已廿六 - '时' means 'time' or 'when'; '已' means 'already'; '廿六' means 'twenty-six'.
  • 众方匿近郭某家 - '众' means 'the crowd' or 'the group'; '方' means 'just' or 'now'; '匿' means 'to hide'; '近郭某家' means 'in a house near a certain Mr. Guo'.
  • 约趁除夕热闹中窜入 - '约' means 'to agree' or 'to plan'; '趁' means 'to take advantage of'; '除夕' means 'New Year's Eve'; '热闹' means 'busy' or 'crowded'; '窜入' means 'to sneak in'.
  • 请见本官告密 - '请' means 'to request'; '见' means 'to meet'; '本官' means 'the current official' or 'the governor'; '告密' means 'to report secretly' or 'to inform on'.
  • 一如大骇 - '一如' means 'as if' or 'as'; '大骇' means 'greatly alarmed' or 'shocked'.
  • 谒大府会营选兵 - '谒' means 'to visit' or 'to pay respects'; '大府' means 'the governor's office'; '会营' means 'to gather troops'; '选兵' means 'to select soldiers'.
  • 聚而缚之 - '聚' means 'to gather'; '缚' means 'to bind' or 'to tie up'.
  • 百余人无一得脱 - '百余人' means 'more than a hundred'; '无一得脱' means 'not a single one escaped'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 委员分鞫 - Refers to a formal legal or investigative process.
  • 诘之号泣 - Means 'questioning him/her with sobs,' indicating an emotional response during interrogation.
  • 身本姓周，年止十四 - Literally translates to 'originally surnamed Zhou, only fourteen years old,' providing personal details of the subject.
  • 业剃发 - Means 'engaged in shaving hair,' likely referring to a profession related to hairdressing or barbering.
  • 杜先生鬻我，易我姓曰朱 - Translates to 'Mr. Du sold me, changed my surname to Zhu,' indicating a change in identity through sale.
  • 使众人拥而拜我 - Means 'made everyone gather and bow to me,' suggesting a ritualistic or ceremonial act.
  • 殊不知所为也 - Translates to 'completely unaware of what was being done,' highlighting the subject's lack of understanding.
  • 顾欲扑我，冤哉！ - Means 'but wanted to hit me, it's so unjust!' expressing the subject's sense of injustice.
  • 盖杜先生所弄狡狯 - Translates to 'it seems Mr. Du played a trick,' indicating deception or manipulation.
  • 计成，将借以号召，托诸蜀王遗裔也 - Means 'the plan succeeded, intending to use it as a rallying cry, claiming descent from the descendants of Shu Wang,' referring to a strategy of using a fabricated lineage for influence.
  • 余亦大半乡愚 - Translates to 'the rest were mostly ignorant villagers,' describing the general level of understanding among the participants.
  • 惟杜先生窃窥断烂朝报 - Means 'only Mr. Du secretly studied the fragmented official records,' suggesting a hidden knowledge or manipulation.
  • 欲效康熙年间邱监生家群奴故智 - Translates to 'intending to imitate the old tricks of the servants of Mr. Qiu, the supervisor during the Kangxi era,' referring to historical precedents of manipulation.
  • 谳者悯其愚，坐杜先生为首 - Means 'the judge pitied their ignorance, sentencing Mr. Du as the leader,' indicating leniency due to perceived naivety.
  • 凌迟，死罪仅四人 - Translates to 'death by slicing, only four people were sentenced to death,' referring to a severe form of punishment.
  • 军流及徒杖数十 - Means 'military exile and dozens of strokes with a stick,' detailing other punishments.
  • 周童得长繁，今遇赦，出狱矣 - Translates to 'the Zhou child grew up, now encounters amnesty, has been released from prison,' indicating a positive outcome for the subject.
  • 相传有李自成嫁母，号流寓旧太后 - Means 'it is said there was a mother-in-law of Li Zicheng, called the former empress dowager in exile,' referencing a historical figure.
  • 威逼草头王 - Translates to 'forcing a grasshopper king,' a metaphorical expression for a small-time leader.
  • 蕞尔乱民 - Means 'such a small group of rebels,' indicating the scale of the rebellion.
  • 亦未尝无对 - Translates to 'it also had its counterpart,' suggesting a balance or response.
  • 杜监绑时，补山师叹曰 - Means 'when Mr. Du was bound, Teacher Bushan sighed,' indicating a moment of reflection.
  • 光天化日之下，不安耕凿，自求族灭，是何肺肠！ - Translates to 'under the bright sky and clear day, not content with farming and building, seeking the extinction of one's clan, what kind of heart does one have!' expressing disapproval.
  • 肖濂雪方群居虐谑，或弈战斗棋失算，不竞辄曰 - Means 'Xiao Lian Xue and others gathered to mock each other, or played chess and lost, not winning they would say,' indicating a pattern of behavior.
  • 杜先生有言，悔之晚矣 - Translates to 'Mr. Du once said, it's too late to regret it,' a common phrase indicating regret after the fact.
  • 柳役赏以百金 - Means 'the Liang official rewarded him with a hundred gold pieces,' indicating a reward.
  • 同一役也，官与民皆阴受其福 - Translates to 'the same official, both officials and civilians secretly benefited from it,' indicating mutual benefit.
  • 与戴如煌所倚仗攫金者，其功罪岂可以道里计哉！ - Translates to 'compared to Dai Ru Huang who relied on seizing gold, how can their merits and faults be measured on the same scale!' expressing a contrast in moral judgment.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天下物之恶 - Literally 'the most detestable things in the world,' used to introduce the subject of opium.
  • 莫恶于 - Means 'nothing is more detestable than,' emphasizing the severity of opium.
  • 鸩毒不啻也 - Means 'not unlike the poison of a sparrowhawk,' a hyperbolic comparison to emphasize the deadly nature of opium.
  • 色恶臭恶、味之恶 - Describes the unpleasant appearance, smell, and taste of opium.
  • 嗜者甘以身殉 - Means 'those who crave it are willing to sacrifice their lives for it,' highlighting the addictive nature of opium.
  • 人之愚莫愚于吸鸦片 - Means 'there is no greater foolishness than smoking opium,' emphasizing the stupidity of opium addiction.
  • 待决之犯，亦无不觊缓须臾 - Means 'even those awaiting execution cannot help but hope for a moment's delay,' illustrating the desperate desire for opium.
  • 临毙之夫，尚无不求延片刻 - Means 'even those on the brink of death still seek to prolong their lives,' further emphasizing the desperation.
  • 吸乌烟者乃日促之 - Means 'those who smoke opium hasten their own demise,' highlighting the destructive nature of opium.
  • 恣纵少年不足惜 - Means 'wasting the youth is not worth lamenting,' suggesting that the loss of young lives is not considered significant.
  • 士大夫亦复为之 - Means 'even scholars and officials partake in it,' indicating the widespread nature of opium addiction.
  • 郑六施七，均以此废且死 - Means 'Zhang Liù and Shi Qī both became addicted and died as a result,' providing specific examples of opium's destructive power.
  • 弥留时惨苦万状 - Means 'in their final moments, they suffered immensely,' describing the agony of dying from opium addiction.
  • 较善终者迥别 - Means 'compared to a peaceful death, the difference is stark,' contrasting the agony of opium-induced death with a natural death.
  • 闻狄都阃言，滇中此时吸者尤多 - Means 'according to Di Dukun, there are many opium users in Yunnan now,' providing a contemporary account of the prevalence of opium.
  • 几于比户有铺矣 - Means 'almost every household has a shop,' emphasizing the widespread availability of opium.
  • 尝见一丰润摺扇，画一躯枯瘠无人状 - Means 'I once saw a fan with a picture of a body so emaciated it seemed lifeless,' describing the physical toll of opium addiction.
  • 共一少妓，翘足并卧炕上，挑灯对吸 - Means 'accompanied by a young courtesan, they lie on the kang, smoking together while a lamp is lit,' depicting the social context of opium use.
  • 两瘦鬼伺其旁闻香嗅气，以手招烟，作垂涎状 - Means 'two thin ghosts stand by, sniffing the air and beckoning for the smoke, drooling with desire,' using personification to describe the allure of opium.
  • 蛇鼠亦僵卧如蛰 - Means 'even snakes and mice lie stiffly, as if hibernating,' using a simile to describe the lifelessness of the environment.
  • 闻烟蠢蠢作亦欲动态 - Means 'they seem to be stirred by the smell of opium, as if they too want to partake,' using a metaphor to describe the pervasive influence of opium.
  • 龙眠妙手，一片救世婆心也 - Means 'the artist's skillful hand, with a heart full of compassion for the world,' attributing the fan's message to the artist's benevolence.
  • 然欲绝之，亦非甚难 - Means 'however, to quit is not very difficult,' suggesting that quitting opium is possible.
  • 予在永嘉，知库书张元龙犯此 - Means 'when I was in Yongjia, I knew that Zhang Yuanlong, the keeper of the treasury, was addicted,' providing another example of opium addiction.
  • 欲绳之，诉曰已绝此二年 - Means 'I wanted to punish him, but he claimed he had quit two years ago,' indicating a false claim of abstinence.
  • 曾以办船料，渡海至苏禄国 - Means 'he once traveled to the Sulu Kingdom to procure ship materials,' providing background on Zhang's travels.
  • 亲见鸦片本质 - Means 'he personally saw the nature of opium,' suggesting that Zhang had firsthand knowledge of opium.
  • 故毅然不敢食耳 - Means 'therefore, he firmly decided not to smoke it,' explaining Zhang's decision to abstain.
  • 询其详，云国俗皆裸葬 - Means 'when I asked for details, he said that in the country, they practice sky burial,' providing cultural context.
  • 一亩之地，百祖共之 - Means 'a single acre of land is shared by a hundred ancestors,' describing a burial custom.
  • 积累百年，其地之值不资矣 - Means 'after a century of accumulation, the value of the land is incalculable,' emphasizing the significance of the burial practice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鸦片 - Refers to opium, a highly addictive and illegal substance that was widely used in China during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 狄都阃 - Refers to Di Dukun, a contemporary figure mentioned in the text.
  • 滇中 - Refers to Yunnan, a province in southwestern China.
  • 丰润摺扇 - Refers to a fan with a picture of a body so emaciated it seemed lifeless, which is used to illustrate the physical toll of opium addiction.
  • 少妓 - Refers to a young courtesan, a common figure in the social context of opium use.
  • 龙眠妙手 - Refers to the artist's skillful hand, attributed to the creator of the fan.
  • 永嘉 - Refers to Yongjia, a place in Zhejiang Province.
  • 库书张元龙 - Refers to Zhang Yuanlong, the keeper of the treasury, who was addicted to opium.

  • 绝之法以十全大补汤加鸦片灰 - Describes a method to stop a habit or addiction using a ten-ingredient tonic with opium ash.

  • This sentence describes two common types of murder cases and the humorous way they are often resolved.
  • This sentence describes a common pattern in murder cases where the victim is portrayed as violent and the perpetrator as gentle, with the weapons being prepared by the victim.
  • This sentence expresses skepticism about the true nature of some cases, even if told by a famous judge.
  • This sentence describes a village in Yunnan where several young children died mysteriously, and the parents suspected a mythical creature.
  • This sentence describes the parents forcing the husband to take drastic action against the suspected creature.
  • This sentence describes the subsequent legal proceedings and the conclusion of the case.
  • This sentence describes a case involving a shaman and a fan with mysterious symbols, leading to the death of the accused.
  • This sentence describes the court's decision and the injustice of the verdict.
  • This sentence describes a particularly complex case involving multiple deaths and families, suggesting that even a famous judge would struggle to resolve it.

  • 平陽案 - A case or incident involving the region of Pingyang (平陽). The term '案' typically refers to a case or investigation.
  • 民风犷悍 - Describes the local customs as fierce and wild. '犷悍' is a compound adjective that emphasizes the rough and tough nature of the people.
  • 每岁催科 - Refers to the annual collection of taxes or levies. '催科' is a term used for the process of collecting taxes.
  • 绅士尤多包揽 - Indicates that the local gentry (绅士) often acted as intermediaries or agents in tax collection. '包揽' means to take charge of or monopolize.
  • 新中武举 - Refers to someone who has recently passed the military examination (武举). '新中' means newly passed, and '武举' is the military examination.
  • 夺多士之所包而悉揽之 - Means to take over what others were handling and take it all for oneself. '夺' means to seize, '多士' refers to many people, '所包' means what they were handling, and '悉揽' means to take all of it.
  • 众不服，讼之 - Indicates that the people did not accept the action and took it to court. '不服' means to不服从, and '讼之' means to take it to court.
  • 呼之不至，捕之则拒 - Describes the resistance shown by the people when called upon or when tried to be arrested. '呼之不至' means they did not come when called, and '捕之则拒' means they resisted when captured.
  • 结硬手许鸿志为羽翼 - Indicates forming a strong alliance with Xu Hongzhi. '结硬手' means to form a strong alliance, and '羽翼' means support or backing.
  • 阖邑畏之，且效之附之 - Means the entire city feared and followed him. '阖邑' means the whole city, '畏之' means to fear him, '效之' means to follow him, and '附之' means to attach themselves to him.
  • 前令蒋澄已七十，乞休去温守杨太鹤惑于庸妄 - Refers to the previous magistrate, Jiang Cheng, who was 70 years old and had retired, and the new governor, Yang Tahe, who was misled by mediocre and foolish people. '乞休' means to request retirement, '去温' means to leave Wen (the place), '惑于庸妄' means to be misled by mediocre and foolish people.
  • 谓非武健不足胜任 - Means that only a strong and capable person could handle the job. '谓' means to say, '非武健' means not strong and capable, and '不足胜任' means not sufficient to handle the job.
  • 访得豫章大挑班徐映台有文武才，乃荐之 - Means that after searching, they found Xu Yingtai from Yuchang, who had both literary and military talents, and recommended him. '访得' means to find out, '大挑班' means a selection group, '文武才' means literary and military talents, and '荐之' means to recommend him.
  • 徐亦觊其可越次也，欣然就之 - Means that Xu Yingtai also hoped to be promoted and gladly accepted the position. '觊' means to hope, '越次' means to be promoted, '欣然' means gladly, and '就之' means to accept the position.
  • 瞰其他往，要而缚之，庄果不能匿，喜甚，亲解赴府 - Means to watch for his absence and then capture him. '瞰' means to watch, '其他往' means his absence, '要而缚之' means to capture him. '庄果不能匿' means Zhuang indeed could not hide, '喜甚' means very happy, and '亲解赴府' means personally taking him to the prefectural office.

  • The exact meaning of *托言倦卧，闭帐取针线纳卷枕中，缝之* is ambiguous. It could mean that the second student claimed to be too tired to get up, closed the curtains, took out some thread and needle, and hid the exam papers in his pillow, where *纳卷枕中* means 'hid the exam papers in his pillow'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 晷已将午 - 晷指日晷，将午指接近中午，表示时间将近中午。
  • 荒厨蔬笋 - 荒厨指简陋的厨房，蔬笋指蔬菜和竹笋，描述食物的简朴。
  • 能共饭乎 - 能共饭乎是礼貌地询问是否可以一起吃饭。
  • 白供净馔数头 - 白是自称谦词，供指提供，净馔指干净的菜肴，数头指几样，表示提供几样干净的菜肴。
  • 绝无酸鹹气也 - 绝无指完全没有，酸鹹指酸咸的味道，气指味道，表示完全没有酸咸的味道。
  • 时方盛暑 - 时方指当时，盛暑指盛夏，表示正值盛夏。
  • 蝇飞如织 - 蝇指苍蝇，飞如织形容苍蝇飞舞的样子像织布一样密集。
  • 侍者挥扇 - 侍者指侍从，挥扇指摇扇子，描述侍从摇扇子以驱热。
  • 旁午 - 旁午指临近中午。
  • 白令另取一钵来 - 白是自称谦词，令指命令，另取指另外取，一钵指一碗，表示命令另外取一碗来。
  • 与客且谈且饭 - 与客指和客人，且谈且饭指边谈边吃，描述与客人边谈边吃饭的情景。
  • 饭已，则钵中之蝇亦满矣 - 饭已指饭吃完，钵中之蝇指碗里的苍蝇，亦满矣指也满了，表示饭吃完后碗里也满了苍蝇。
  • 众大诧叹 - 众指众人，大诧叹指大为惊讶并感叹，描述众人的反应。
  • 无庸 - 无庸指不需要或没关系。
  • 不过指头活脱耳 - 不过指只是，指头活脱指手指灵活，耳指而已，表示只是手指灵活而已。
  • 本无勇也，安所得神 - 本无勇也指本来没有勇气，安所得神指哪里来的神通，表示没有神通。
  • 旧曾学一小技 - 旧曾指过去曾经，学一小技指学过一点小技巧，表示过去学过一点小技巧。
  • 未知今尚能否 - 未知指不知道，今尚能否指现在还能不能，表示不知道现在还能不能做到。
  • 引入一精舍 - 引入指带领进入，一精舍指一个小庙或书房，表示带领客人进入一个小庙或书房。
  • 舍中新铺方砖 - 舍中指屋内，新铺指新铺设，方砖指方形砖，表示屋内新铺设了方形砖。
  • 胶以灰沙 - 胶以指用胶粘，灰沙指灰浆，表示用灰浆粘贴。
  • 殊甚坚緻 - 殊甚指非常，坚緻指坚固细腻，表示非常坚固细腻。
  • 白方跣着棕鞋 - 白方指我方，跣着指赤脚穿着，棕鞋指棕榈鞋，表示我方赤脚穿着棕榈鞋。
  • 自南至北，倚墙微步一周 - 自南至北指从南到北，倚墙指靠墙，微步一周指缓慢走一圈，表示从南到北靠墙缓慢走一圈。
  • 则阖室之砖皆起矣 - 则指于是，阖室之砖指整个房间的砖，皆起矣指都起来了，表示整个房间的砖都起来了。
  • 谓客曰 - 谓客曰指对客人说。
  • 此砖铺本未匀，下多轩轾 - 此砖铺指这些砖，本未匀指本来就不均匀，下多轩轾指下面有很多高低不平，表示这些砖本来就不均匀，下面有很多高低不平。
  • 省呼拙匠又需一日工也 - 省呼指省得叫，拙匠指笨拙的工匠，又需一日工也指又要一天的功夫，表示省得叫笨拙的工匠又要一天的功夫。
  • 众者吐舌，膜拜而散 - 众者指众人，吐舌指吐舌头，膜拜而散指膜拜后散去，表示众人吐舌头膜拜后散去。
  • 白僧郡人多见之 - 白僧指白僧，郡人指郡中人，多见之指常常见到，表示白僧常常见到郡中人。
  • 且述其轶事甚伙 - 且述指并且讲述，其轶事指他的轶事，甚伙指很多，表示并且讲述他的许多轶事。
  • 予生已晚，不及睹矣 - 予指我，生已晚指出生太晚，不及睹矣指没能见到，表示我出生太晚没能见到。

  • 寒暖相背也 - The cold and warm are opposite, possibly referring to the widow's emotional state or the conflicting forces at play.
  • 保定署中有废圃 - Description of an abandoned garden in the Baoding office, setting the scene for the next part of the story.
  • 一狐据焉 - A fox took residence there, introducing a new element of the narrative.
  • 夜必以鸡子七枚、火酒一尊享之乃不祟 - Ritual described where the fox is offered seven eggs and a jug of fire wine to prevent it from causing trouble.
  • 晨视之，酒与卵俱空矣 - Morning inspection where both the wine and eggs are found empty, indicating that the ritual was successful.
  • 法官过，乃乞一符榜于楹 - Magistrate passing by and asking for a talisman to be posted on the door, suggesting a superstitious or ritualistic act.
  • 此夜二簋完然 - Night when two bowls remained intact, implying that the ritual was effective.
  • 惟武林所见法官，术尤异 - Magistrate seen in Wulin (West Lake), noting his particularly unusual skills.
  • 友人俞君述之 - Friend named Yu recounts the story, providing a personal connection to the events.
  • 言其葭莩戚叶姓，家苏之西洞庭 - Description of the magistrate's relative, mentioning his name and his residence in the western part of Dongting Lake.
  • 以贩缯五业，勾留在楚 - Relative's occupation as a silk trader and his residence in Chu (a historical region), providing context for his background.
  • 所居爨室中忽见绣履一双，离地尺许，步虚而行 - Strange occurrence where a pair of embroidered shoes appeared to walk in the air, adding a supernatural element to the story.
  • 越翌日，复见湘裙六幅笼其上，自腰以上则熟视终无睹也 - Continuation of the strange occurrence, where a six-panel Xiang skirt appeared to cover the shoes, and the upper part remained invisible.
  • 劻勷兼旬 - Events lasting for two months, indicating the duration of the strange occurrences.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 法官 - Magistrate or judge, a figure of authority in traditional Chinese society.
  • 妖 - Ghost or supernatural being, often associated with misfortune or malevolent forces.
  • 冤 - Wronged spirit, often a victim of injustice or unfulfilled desires.
  • 保定 - Baoding, a city in Hebei Province, China.
  • 狐 - Fox, often associated with trickery or supernatural abilities in Chinese folklore.
  • 火酒 - Fire wine, a type of strong alcohol used in rituals or offerings.
  • 符榜 - Talisman or charm, often used in traditional Chinese practices to ward off evil or bring good fortune.
  • 武林 - Wulin, an area around West Lake in Hangzhou, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 葭莩戚叶 - Relative by marriage or a distant family connection, emphasizing the personal nature of the story.
  • 苏之西洞庭 - Western part of Dongting Lake in Suzhou, a region known for its natural beauty and cultural heritage.
  • 贩缯 - Silk trading, a common occupation in ancient China, especially in regions like Dongting Lake.
  • 楚 - Chu, a historical region in central and southern China, known for its rich cultural and political history.
  • 绣履 - Embroidered shoes, often associated with elegance and refinement in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 湘裙 - Xiang skirt, a type of traditional Chinese skirt, often associated with the Xiang River region.
  • 劻勷 - Period of time, often used to describe a prolonged or extended duration.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 而葉生歸 - When Ye returned home.
  • 家人方聚訴 - His family was just gathering to complain.
  • 一偕歸友大言曰 - A friend who returned with him spoke loudly.
  • 此必有物憑焉 - There must be a spirit possessing him.
  • 當以道士驅之 - We should use a Taoist priest to exorcise it.
  • 圍屏上即嘤聲應曰 - Suddenly, a voice replied from the screen.
  • 何必道士，看汝先做和尚 - Why bother with a Taoist? Watch you become a monk first.
  • 即火起帽中，燎毛灼膚 - Suddenly, fire started in his hat, scorching his hair and skin.
  • 鼠竄而去 - He ran away like a rat.
  • 葉愈忿 - Ye became even more furious.
  • 吾歸途聞天師在杭，亟往訴之 - I heard on my way back that the Celestial Master is in Hangzhou, so I went to report it immediately.
  • 即留原船發櫂 - He immediately left the original boat and set sail.
  • 屏上復作聲曰 - The screen made another sound and said.
  • 汝敢行，吾即取汝子 - If you dare to go, I will take your son.
  • 時子方遊戲堂前，蹶然而倒 - At that time, his son was playing in front of the hall and suddenly fell down.
  • 葉更毅然不顧曰 - Ye became even more resolute and disregarded it, saying.
  • 有命在天，妖何能為 - It is fate in heaven; how can an evil spirit do anything?
  • 絕裾而去 - He tore his robe and left.
  • 至杭，投一牒，饬一法官往治 - When he arrived in Hangzhou, he submitted a petition and instructed a magistrate to handle it.
  • 例需四十金，葉倍奉之，恳其速往 - It usually required forty taels of silver, but Ye doubled the amount and urged him to go quickly.
  • 先选二力士，用黄巾缚首，行缠束足，手三股叉，又取一鹤氅衣 - He first selected two strong men, tied yellow turbans around their heads, bound their feet, and gave them three-pronged spears, and also took a crane robe.
  • 叶遍身符篆，一夕而至，择于厅事结壇 - Ye covered himself with talismans, arrived in one night, and chose a place in the hall to set up an altar.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 葉生 - A proper noun referring to a person named Ye.
  • 天師 - Refers to the Celestial Master, a title given to leaders of the Way of the Celestial Masters, a Taoist sect.
  • 苏州 - The modern city of Suzhou, another significant cultural and economic center in Jiangsu Province.
  • 土神 - Local gods or deities believed to protect specific areas or communities.
  • 霤 - Spirits believed to inhabit the household, often associated with the roof or eaves.
  • 天罗地网 - A metaphor for an inescapable trap or net, often used in literature to describe a situation where escape is impossible.
